findings.description: ['SE16N', 'SM30', 'SE16N'] used in firefighter session. These tcodes allow direct table editing, bypassing normal authorization objects and change management. Requires a documented data-fix request to be compliant.

Segregation of Duties (SoD) violation: vendor master maintenance and payment execution combined in one session. This allows a single person to change vendor bank details and immediately trigger a payment to the new account.
Direct modification of vendor bank data (table LFBK, field BANKN). This is a critical fraud-risk change: altering bank account details can redirect payments. Must be approved by AP management.

The firefighter user and the ticket requester are the same person (BMEYER). This is a self-approval pattern: the person who requested emergency access also performed the work, with no independent oversight.
